title: AI Engineering (Chip Huyen, 2025)
description: This book introduces the emerging field of AI engineering, which focuses on building real-world applications powered by foundation models rather than training models from scratch. Chip Huyen, drawing on her industry and academic experience, provides a practical guide to evaluation, prompt design, retrieval-augmented generation, fine-tuning, dataset curation, and optimization. It serves as both a roadmap and toolkit for engineers transitioning from traditional ML to modern AI systems.

chapters.title: Introduction to Building AI Applications with Foundation Models
chapters.summary: Learn the foundations of AI engineering, foundation models, generative AI systems, self-supervised learning, multimodal AI, and how modern AI applications are built using pretrained models and APIs.

chapters.title: Understanding Foundation Models
chapters.summary: Learn how foundation models work internally including Transformers, attention mechanisms, scaling laws, mixture-of-experts architectures, multilingual modeling, supervised fine-tuning, and alignment techniques.

chapters.title: Evaluation Methodology
chapters.summary: Learn how to evaluate AI systems using perplexity, semantic similarity, exact match metrics, AI-as-a-judge systems, benchmark analysis, and evaluation frameworks for generative AI.

chapters.title: Evaluate AI Systems
chapters.summary: Learn enterprise AI evaluation techniques including hallucination detection, factual consistency, toxicity analysis, latency evaluation, conversational testing, and evaluation-driven development workflows.

chapters.title: Prompt Engineering
chapters.summary: Learn systematic prompt engineering techniques including zero-shot and few-shot prompting, chain-of-thought reasoning, self-critique prompting, prompt optimization, and reliable instruction design.

chapters.title: RAG and Agents
chapters.summary: Learn Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), semantic retrieval, vector databases, chunking strategies, query rewriting, AI agents, tool usage, orchestration systems, and external memory architectures.

chapters.title: Finetuning
chapters.summary: Learn how to adapt foundation models using supervised fine-tuning, preference tuning, LoRA, quantization-aware training, parameter-efficient finetuning, and transfer learning strategies.

chapters.title: Dataset Engineering
chapters.summary: Learn how datasets for AI systems are designed, cleaned, generated, augmented, annotated, and optimized for coverage, diversity, quality, and large-scale model training.

chapters.title: Inference Optimization
chapters.summary: Learn how to optimize AI inference systems using quantization, batching, speculative decoding, KV cache optimization, FlashAttention, GPU optimization, and hardware-aware deployment strategies.

chapters.title: AI Engineering Architecture and User Feedback
chapters.summary: Learn how production AI systems are architected including routing systems, observability, monitoring, caching, guardrails, feedback loops, user analytics, and scalable AI infrastructure.

title: Hands-On Large Language Models (Jay Alammar & Maarten Grootendorst, 2024)
description: Written by two well-known educators in the AI community, this book offers a visual and hands-on journey into large language models. It blends intuitive explanations, diagrams, and Python code to show how LLMs work internally and how to apply them for classification, clustering, generation, retrieval, and fine-tuning. The book emphasizes accessibility and practical experimentation, making it a strong resource for both beginners and practitioners.

chapters.title: An Introduction to Large Language Models
chapters.summary: Learn the evolution of NLP and LLMs including Transformers, attention mechanisms, transfer learning, encoder vs decoder architectures, and the foundations of modern language AI.

chapters.title: Tokens and Embeddings
chapters.summary: Learn how LLMs process text using tokenization, vocabularies, token IDs, contextual embeddings, subword algorithms, and semantic vector representations.

chapters.title: Looking Inside Large Language Models
chapters.summary: Learn how Transformer-based LLMs generate text using autoregressive decoding, attention layers, Transformer blocks, sampling methods, KV caching, and context management.

chapters.title: Text Classification
chapters.summary: Learn how language models perform classification tasks including sentiment analysis, embedding-based classification, zero-shot classification, evaluation metrics, and supervised NLP systems.

chapters.title: Text Clustering and Topic Modeling
chapters.summary: Learn semantic clustering, topic modeling, BERTopic, UMAP, HDBSCAN, embedding-based grouping, and unsupervised organization of text data.

chapters.title: Prompt Engineering
chapters.summary: Learn prompt engineering strategies including few-shot prompting, chain-of-thought reasoning, self-consistency prompting, tree-of-thought reasoning, and generation control techniques.

chapters.title: Advanced Text Generation Techniques and Tools
chapters.summary: Learn advanced LLM orchestration using LangChain, conversational memory, AI agents, tool usage, workflows, chains, and generation pipelines.

chapters.title: Semantic Search and Retrieval-Augmented Generation
chapters.summary: Learn semantic search systems, vector databases, dense retrieval, reranking, chunking strategies, ANN indexing, and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).

chapters.title: Multimodal Large Language Models
chapters.summary: Learn multimodal AI systems including Vision Transformers, CLIP, BLIP-2, multimodal embeddings, image-text alignment, and vision-language architectures.

chapters.title: Creating Text Embedding Models
chapters.summary: Learn how embedding models are trained using contrastive learning, Siamese networks, cosine similarity, ranking losses, and semantic representation learning.

chapters.title: Fine-Tuning Representation Models for Classification
chapters.summary: Learn how representation models like BERT are fine-tuned for classification tasks using few-shot learning, domain adaptation, layer freezing, and contrastive learning techniques.

chapters.title: Fine-Tuning Generation Models
chapters.summary: Learn how generative LLMs are adapted using supervised fine-tuning, LoRA, QLoRA, chat templates, instruction tuning, quantization, and alignment strategies.

title: Hands-On Machine Learning (Aurélien Géron, 2019/2020)
description: Aurélien Géron's widely acclaimed guide teaches machine learning through an applied, example-first approach using Python libraries like Scikit-Learn, TensorFlow, and Keras. Starting from fundamental concepts such as classification and decision trees, it progresses toward deep learning topics like CNNs, RNNs, and reinforcement learning. The book is designed as a comprehensive, practice-driven reference for students and professionals aiming to build robust ML systems.

chapters.title: The Machine Learning Landscape
chapters.summary: Learn the foundations of machine learning, including supervised and unsupervised learning, reinforcement learning, online vs batch learning, model-based learning, and the complete ML workflow.

chapters.title: End-to-End Machine Learning Project
chapters.summary: Learn how to build machine learning projects from data collection and preprocessing to feature engineering, pipelines, evaluation, and hyperparameter tuning.

chapters.title: Classification
chapters.summary: Learn supervised classification systems, confusion matrices, precision/recall, ROC curves, multiclass classification, and evaluation techniques.

chapters.title: Training Models
chapters.summary: Learn linear regression, gradient descent, regularization, polynomial regression, optimization methods, and the mathematics behind machine learning training.

chapters.title: Support Vector Machines
chapters.summary: Learn SVMs, margins, support vectors, kernel methods, nonlinear classification, and regression using support vector techniques.

chapters.title: Decision Trees
chapters.summary: Learn how decision trees work using entropy, Gini impurity, CART algorithms, pruning, regression trees, and interpretable AI methods.

chapters.title: Ensemble Learning and Random Forests
chapters.summary: Learn bagging, boosting, random forests, AdaBoost, gradient boosting, ensemble systems, and variance reduction techniques.

chapters.title: Dimensionality Reduction
chapters.summary: Learn PCA, manifold learning, t-SNE, feature compression, latent spaces, and visualization of high-dimensional data.

chapters.title: Unsupervised Learning Techniques
chapters.summary: Learn clustering, anomaly detection, Gaussian mixture models, DBSCAN, expectation-maximization, and unsupervised pattern discovery.

chapters.title: Introduction to Artificial Neural Networks with Keras
chapters.summary: Learn perceptrons, multilayer neural networks, activation functions, backpropagation, Keras APIs, and deep learning foundations.

chapters.title: Training Deep Neural Networks
chapters.summary: Learn advanced deep learning training techniques including transfer learning, gradient stabilization, initialization strategies, normalization, and optimization tricks.

chapters.title: Custom Models and Training with TensorFlow
chapters.summary: Learn TensorFlow internals, GradientTape, custom losses, custom metrics, custom layers, subclassing, autodiff, and custom training loops.

chapters.title: Loading and Preprocessing Data with TensorFlow
chapters.summary: Learn scalable TensorFlow data pipelines using tf.data, TFRecord, preprocessing layers, batching, shuffling, and efficient dataset systems.

chapters.title: Deep Computer Vision Using Convolutional Neural Networks
chapters.summary: Learn CNNs, convolution operations, pooling, feature maps, ResNet, transfer learning, and deep learning for computer vision.

chapters.title: Processing Sequences Using RNNs and CNNs
chapters.summary: Learn RNNs, LSTMs, GRUs, sequence modeling, time series forecasting, and temporal deep learning architectures.

chapters.title: Natural Language Processing with RNNs and Attention
chapters.summary: Learn tokenization, embeddings, attention mechanisms, sequence-to-sequence systems, Transformers, and NLP generation architectures.

chapters.title: Autoencoders, GANs, and Diffusion Models
chapters.summary: Learn autoencoders, latent spaces, VAEs, GANs, denoising systems, and generative AI architectures.

chapters.title: Reinforcement Learning
chapters.summary: Learn reinforcement learning, policies, rewards, Q-learning, Bellman equations, policy gradients, and autonomous AI agents.

chapters.title: Training and Deploying TensorFlow Models at Scale
chapters.summary: Learn model deployment, TensorFlow Serving, Vertex AI, inference optimization, TensorFlow Lite, scalable serving systems, and production AI deployment.
